info.title: mynumbers-cdr
info.description:
The following methods allows you to request your Call Detail Records (CDRs), request the status of your requests and download your CDRs.
The CDRs are available for both Inbound and Outbound calls terminated/originated to/from BICS numbers as from the 1st of January 2020.
The CDRs for Global Mobile Numbers are available for both Inbound and Outbound calls as from 1st of February 2023.

API Flow

We advise you to implement the following flow for each CDR Request. ![API Flow](data:image/png;base64,)

IMPORTANT

  • The results of your 12 last requests are available for download.
  • Depending on your request parameters, it can take some time until the file becomes available for download.
  • The CDRs of day-1 are available as from 16:00 CET.
  • The call timestamps are adjusted to Daylight Savings Time.
  • It is possible that some attributes are not available for specific CDRs (such as access number if the number was not purchased on BICS portal/API, etc.).
  • For some CDR records, the 'Origin Country' attribute is marked with 'UKN' (Unknown) when it cannot be identified.
  • Some Inbound CDRs may have in the 'b-number' attribute a prefix starting with '00xxxx' or 'xxxx' which may be due to reference data discrepancy in BICS' systems. In such cases, the 'access number' attribute will not be displayed but could be deducted by extracting the last digits of the 'b-number' attribute.

What are the errors that MyNumbers API can return ?

| Code | Description | Http response | | --- | --- | --- | | E001 | The product does not exist in our system. | 400 | | E002 | The country does not exist in our system. | 400 | | E018 | The product is not yet handled via our API. | 400 | | E020 | Please fill out all the mandatory attributes. | 400 | | E030 | The service is temporarily unavailable. Please retry later or contact BICS. | 500 | | E031 | The media type in your header is invalid. Please use application/json. | 400 | | E081 | Request payload is invalid. | 400 | | E086 | Invalid value for parameter {attribute}. | 400 | | E088 | Date range not valid. | 400 | | E090 | Your request is expired. | 404 | | E094 | Your request failed. Please create new request. | 404 | | E095 | Your request is not completed yet. Please try later. | 404 | | E096 | There is no CDR matching the parameters of your request. | 404 | | E117 | This API does not support product {product}. | 400 | | E148 | Direction is not allowed for this product. | 400 |

tags.name: CDR
tags.description: The following methods allows you to request your Call Detail Records (CDRs), request the status of your requests and download your CDRs. The CDRs are available for both Inbound and Outbound calls terminated/originated to/from BICS numbers as from the 1st of January  2020.

get.description: This method allows you download CDR requests.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
allowed values are
  • "json" - default value if not specified in the request
  • "csv" - values are separated by semi-colon

schema.description:
allowed values are
  • "json" - default value if not specified in the request
  • "csv" - values are separated by semi-colon

400.description: Bad request, An issue occurred while processing the input parameters.

404.description: Not found, verify url and/or id used in the request.

get.description: This method allows you to request the status of your CDR request.

404.description: Not found, verify url and/or id used in the request.

get.description: This method allows you to verify upfront if CDR data is available for a specific date range.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Filter by from date. Not earlier than today – 1 year. The format of the date is YYYY-MM-DD.

schema.description: Filter by from date. Not earlier than today – 1 year. The format of the date is YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: Filter by to date. Not earlier than today – 1 year. The format of the date is YYYY-MM-DD.

schema.description: Filter by to date. Not earlier than today – 1 year. The format of the date is YYYY-MM-DD.

400.description: Bad request, An issue occurred while processing the input parameters.
Bad request, An issue occurred while processing the input parameters.

status.description:
Status of your request. Possible values are
  • "Requested" - your request is accepted and wait processing
  • "Executing" - your request is being executed
  • "Done" - your request is complete, use method CDR download to get the result
  • "Error" - there was an error processing your request
  • "Expired" - your request is expired, the file is not available anymore
  • "No results" - your request is complete, no CDRs found

releaseCauseCode.description: Release cause code. (see APPENDIX A – Release Cause codes)

product.description:
Valid product codes. Possible values
  • "IBN" for Cloud Numbers
  • "ITFS" for International Freephone Numbers
  • "GMN" for Global Mobile Numbers
  • "UIFN" for Universal International Freephone Numbers
  • "SCS" for Shared Cost Services

aCountry.description: Origin country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

include0Duration.description:
Include CDRs where the call duration is 0. Possible values are
  • true
  • false (default value if not specified in the request)

bCountry.description: Destination country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

toDate.description:
Maximum 31 days after 'fromDate'
e.g.: 1 May till 31 May or 15 June till 15 July

direction.description:
Valid options are
  • "IN" for inbound traffic (default value if not specified in the request)
  • "OUT" for outbound. Not available for UIFN and SCS products

calendar.description: Pricing Calendar Reference. See Get Calendar and Get Calendar by Product.

releaseCauseCode.description: code why the call was closed (see also APPENDIX A – Release Cause codes)

product.description: Product code,  IBN for cloud number, ITFS for International Freephone Number, "UIFN" for Universal International Freephone Numbers, "SCS" for Shared Cost Services

sipCallId.description: Global unique identifier for the call.

durationSeconds.description: Duration in seconds of the call

profile.description: Tariff differentiation within a country for Inbound Traffic. It intervenes in the billing.

destinationCountry.description: Destination country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format

ibisCode.description: Tariff differentiation within a country for Outbound Traffic. It intervenes in the billing.

releaseCauseDescription.description: Description of the close of call code

accessType.description:
Access type of the calling party. The possible values are
  • "Fix" - Fix line
  • "Mob" - Mobile line
  • "Pay" - Payphone line
  • "fixMob" - Fix and Mobile lines
  • "fixPay" - Fix and Payphone lines
  • "mobPay" - Mobile and Payphone lines
  • "fixMobPay" - Fix, Mobile and Payphone lines

durationMinutes.description: Duration in minutes of the call

customerPoP.description: Point of Presence (PoP) refers to your access point to which the traffic is routed

tde.description:
Time division element that differentiates the price based on the day and time of the call.
  • STD - Standard
  • P - Peak
  • OP - Off Peak
  • OOP - Off Off Peak
  • WED - Weekend

callTimestamp.description: Start date and time of the call in format "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" in CET (Central European Time)

originCountry.description: Origin country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format

direction.description:
possible values are
  • IN for inbound traffic
  • OUT for outbound traffic. Not available for UIFN and SCS products

status.description:
Available date status. Possible values are
  • `0` - All cdrs for this date are available
  • `1` - Cdr data is partially available
  • `2` - No cdr data available

code.description: Error code, the complete description can be found in API documentation

description.description: Description of the error, the complete description can be found in API documentation
description.example: Description of the error that occurred.

timestamp.description: timestamp of error in UTC format

status.description:
Status of your request. Possible values are
  • "Requested" - your request is accepted and wait processing
  • "Executing" - your request is being executed
  • "Done" - your request is complete, use method CDR download to get the result
  • "Error" - there was an error processing your request
  • "Expired" - your request is expired, the file is not available anymore
  • "No results" - your request is complete, no CDRs found
